context: Pre-market Saturday, July 18: a risk-off, range-bound tape in Extreme Fear (F&G 25, down from 27), with BTC dominance firm at 56.4% — capital is sheltering in Bitcoin while alts bleed (SOL -4% and XRP -2% on the week). BTC (~$64.0k) is pressing the top of a tight $62.5k–$64.3k 24h range; there is no trend and summer liquidity is thin. The week's dominant driver is legislative, not macro: this week's CPI/PPI are already released and no high-impact US data prints in the July 20 week, so the binary is the CLARITY Act Senate floor vote — Majority Leader Thune has pledged a vote before the August recess with the July 20 week under active discussion, but Polymarket passage odds have fallen to ~34% after three Senate Democrats publicly opposed the merged draft on July 17. XRP (~$1.086) is the name to watch: whales accumulated ~70M tokens into exchange-reserve lows even as price sits pinned between $1.10 supply and the $1.00 psychological floor — an accumulation-vs-price divergence into a binary. Plan: fade range extremes, don't chase the middle, and size for a two-way gap on any CLARITY headline. Themes in play: Regulatory-US (lead), Macro-Risk, ETF & Flows, Whale & Smart Money, L1 Rotation.

tickers.thesis: XRP sits at $1.086, pinned between the $1.10 round-number supply cap and the $1.00 psychological floor while the CLARITY Act's fate is decided in the Senate this coming week. The tradeable tension is a divergence: whales scooped ~70M tokens in the past week and exchange reserves hit fresh lows (accumulation), futures OI is rebuilding toward $2.5B — yet spot bleeds and Polymarket passage odds fell to ~34% after three Senate Democrats publicly opposed the merged draft on July 17. Acceptance back above $1.10 opens $1.13 then the $1.20 shelf and confirms the accumulation read; rejection there or a daily close below $1.07 exposes $1.02 and the $1.00 line, where the heaviest long-liquidation and capitulation interest sits. A headline-driven name into a binary — size for a two-way gap.
levels: Recent swing high; where the last bounce failed

setup.entry: Long only on a sweep of the $1.07 24h low + reclaim, or a break-and-retest of $1.10 with volume — do not chase mid-range
setup.stop: Below $1.055 (1.5% under the 24h-low sweep); no long survives a daily close under $1.05
setup.tp2: $1.13 then $1.20 runner if a CLARITY floor vote is scheduled
setup.invalidation: Daily close below $1.05, OR the CLARITY floor vote is pulled from the July 20 week (removes the near-term catalyst)

catalysts: CLARITY Act Senate floor vote — Majority Leader Thune pledged a vote before the August recess; week of July 20 under active discussion. Passage odds ~34% and falling after Sens. Murphy, Van Hollen & Merkley opposed the merged draft over dropped ethics provisions (verified Jul 17-18)
Whale accumulation: ~70M XRP bought in the past week, exchange reserves at fresh lows, futures OI rebuilding toward $2.5B (CME +0.74% 24h) — accumulation into weakness
Binance $800k XRP airdrop for RLUSD holders running Jul 17 – Aug 14 (weekly Friday distributions) — mild demand tailwind
liq_above: Short-liq cluster at the round-number cap

tickers.thesis: BTC is pressing the top of its 24h range at ~$63,990 after rebounding off $62,528, while the market sits in Extreme Fear (F&G 25, down from 27) and dominance holds firm at 56.4% — capital is hiding in Bitcoin while alts bleed. The decision point is immediate: acceptance above the $64,300 24h high opens a run at the $65,000 psychological level and the $66,000 supply shelf; failure to break, or a slip back under $62,500, re-opens $61,000 and the $60,000 round number where the week's long-liquidation risk concentrates. No trend, thin summer liquidity and a regulatory-driven tape — a range to fade at the extremes, not chase in the middle.

setup.entry: Range play — long a sweep-and-reclaim of $62,500, or short a rejection of $64,300–$65,000; no position in the $63k mid-range
setup.stop: Longs below $61,800; shorts above $65,300 (beyond the round number)
setup.invalidation: Daily close outside the $62.5k–$64.3k range flips the plan to breakout-follow, not fade

catalysts: Extreme Fear (F&G 25) + 56.4% BTC dominance — risk-off rotation into BTC; a sentiment reset toward neutral would be the bounce trigger
Macro-light week — CPI/PPI already released (Jul 14-15); no scheduled high-impact US print in the Jul 20 week. Next Fed event is the late-July FOMC (Jul 28-29)
CLARITY Act outcome is a market-wide risk proxy — a Senate floor-vote failure would pressure the whole complex, BTC included

tickers.thesis: SOL is the relative laggard (-4.07% on the week) and sits just below the $76–$82 zone that has flipped from support to overhead resistance. The bullish structural story is intact underneath: US spot SOL ETFs logged net inflows on every July trading day, AUM crossed $1B, Morgan Stanley amended its filing to a market-low 0.14% fee, and Solana just took the #1 RWA-holder spot (300k+). Near-term the read is level-mechanical — reclaiming $76 puts it back inside the zone toward $78/$82; losing the $73.45 24h low opens $70 and the psychological floor. Structural demand vs a broken short-term level: accumulate weakness, don't chase into resistance.

setup.entry: Long a sweep-and-reclaim of $73.45, or a reclaim of $76 with volume for continuation; no chase between
setup.invalidation: Daily close below $72 cedes the range to $70/$68

tickers.thesis: SUI is coiling in a tight $0.72–$0.77 range after shedding ~63% from its January $1.96 high — the quietest name on the board today (flat on 24h). Analysts flag $0.77 as the pivot that decides direction overhead and $0.71 as the floor. The overhang is idiosyncratic: DEX BlueMove allegedly withdrew ~700k SUI on July 12, a security scare that capped the last bounce. Structural support (US spot ETFs live, ~74% of supply staked) is intact but not being priced today. Level-mechanical — break and hold $0.77 opens $0.82; loss of the $0.72 24h low / $0.71 floor opens $0.67. A coil into a break: wait for the resolution, don't pre-position.
levels: Analysts' line in the sand overhead

setup.entry: Long a reclaim of $0.72 after a sweep, or a break-and-retest of $0.77; short only on a clean rejection of $0.77
setup.stop: Longs below $0.705 (under the floor); shorts above $0.785
setup.invalidation: Daily close below $0.71 opens $0.67; close above $0.77 flips bias up to $0.82

catalysts: BlueMove DEX ~700k SUI withdrawal (Jul 12) — unresolved security/confidence overhang capping bounces
US spot SUI ETFs live (21Shares TSUI, Grayscale, Canary) + ~74% of supply staked — structural floor, not a near-term catalyst
Monthly token-unlock cadence — recurring supply pressure to track on the calendar (Token Unlocks theme)

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 25 (Extreme Fear), Δ -2 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 25 (Extreme Fear) at 10:09Z — -2 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: XRP: whales buy the fear while the Street sells the odds
insights.body: The non-obvious read on XRP is a divergence, not a direction. Wallets scooped ~70M XRP in the past week and exchange reserves hit fresh lows (accumulation), futures OI is rebuilding toward $2.5B with CME +0.74% on the day — yet spot sits at $1.086 in Extreme Fear and Polymarket's CLARITY passage odds fell to ~34%. Smart money is positioning for a bill the betting market is fading. That gap resolves violently on the floor-vote headline; the accumulation says lean long on a $1.07 reclaim, but only with a stop that survives a $1.00 flush.

insights.title: The binary is a vote, not a print
insights.body: Unusually for a summer week there is no CPI/NFP/FOMC to trade — those already cleared (CPI Jul 14, PPI Jul 15). The only scheduled inflection is the CLARITY Act Senate floor vote, which Thune has floated for the week of July 20. Three Democrats (Murphy, Van Hollen, Merkley) formally opposed the merged draft on July 17 over dropped ethics provisions, and the bill still needs ~7 Democratic votes / 60 to advance. Treat a vote-scheduling headline as the catalyst and a vote-pulled headline as the invalidation — for XRP specifically and the whole complex as a risk proxy.

insights.title: SOL: the tape is red, the flows are green
insights.body: SOL is the weekly laggard (-4%) sitting below the $76–82 zone that flipped to resistance, but the institutional bid never left: US spot SOL ETFs took inflows on every July trading day, AUM crossed $1B, Morgan Stanley cut its filing to a market-low 0.14% fee, and Solana just became the #1 RWA-holder chain. Price-vs-flow divergence favors accumulating $73–74 weakness over chasing; the Alpenglow Q3 upgrade is the structural catalyst that can front-run activation. Reclaim $76 to confirm.

insights.title: Extreme Fear without a reclaim is not a bottom
insights.body: F&G at 25 (down from 27) is a condition, not a signal. BTC is holding a tight range and dominance is climbing — classic risk-off sheltering, not capitulation-and-reversal. The bottom-zone confluence (swing-clock + volume + divergence) that the Reversal Radar needs isn't present yet. Don't buy 'oversold' in a vacuum; wait for a reclaim of a broken level (BTC $64.3k, SOL $76, XRP $1.10) before treating fear as a fade.

insights.title: SUI is a coil with an idiosyncratic overhang
insights.body: SUI is flat and coiling in $0.72–0.77, but the reason it can't bounce is name-specific: the ~700k SUI BlueMove withdrawal on July 12 is an unresolved confidence hit that keeps capping rallies at the $0.77 pivot. Structural support (spot ETFs, ~74% staked) is real but dormant. A wait-for-the-break name — the $0.77 reclaim or $0.71 loss tells you which way; don't pre-position the coil.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 25 (Extreme Fear), Δ -2 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm
HackerNews trending crypto-tagged: "AI Meets Cryptography 2: What AI Found in OpenVM's ZkVM" (↑94)

Whale accumulation (XRP): ~70M XRP bought in the past week per on-chain trackers, exchange reserves at fresh lows and rising >1M-token Coinbase outflows — smart-money accumulation into price weakness. Implication: leans long-XRP on a sweep-reclaim, contra the falling CLARITY odds.

CME XRP open interest: total XRP futures OI near $2.5B, +2.65% 24h with CME specifically +0.74% — fresh institutional capital entering derivatives at the lows, not exiting. Implication: positioning, not capitulation.

ETF-flow streak (SOL): US spot Solana ETFs logged net inflows on every July trading day and AUM crossed $1B — a persistent bid invisible on the price chart (SOL still -4% on the week). Implication: accumulate weakness rather than read the red candle literally.

Senate whip signal: three Democrats (Murphy, Van Hollen, Merkley) held a press conference formally opposing the CLARITY merged draft on July 17 — a pre-vote whip-count tell that the ~7 needed Democratic votes aren't yet there. Implication: floor-vote odds (~34%) are more likely to fall than rise absent an ethics-provision compromise.

Binance RLUSD airdrop (XRP): $800k in XRP distributed weekly to RLUSD holders Jul 17 – Aug 14. Implication: a small mechanical demand tailwind and a reason for retail to hold RLUSD-linked balances rather than sell.

Fear delta: F&G ticked 27 → 25 (deeper Extreme Fear) even as BTC rose intraday, with dominance rising — sentiment lagging price. Implication: risk-off sheltering in BTC, not a broad risk-appetite recovery; alt bounces stay sell-the-rip until a level reclaims.
